concerns.text: T018 mandates running Mann-Whitney U test but omits the required multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg) explicitly mandated by SC-001. This is a silent relaxation of the success criterion, rendering the statistical validation incomplete as defined in the spec.

T022 describes constructing triplets to predict a 'correctable' label but fails to specify the implementation of the 'Semantic Outcome Oracle' (manual expert annotation or rule-based oracle) required by FR-002 to derive the ground truth. Without this task, the dataset construction violates the spec's ground-truth generation constraint.

T030 implements a 'Rule-Based Rewriter' for syntactic fixes. This contradicts FR-004 and US-3, which require the adapter to learn a 'generalizable correction policy based on abstract fix representations' rather than relying on a separate deterministic rule engine. The task silently shifts the architectural constraint from a learned policy to a heuristic fallback.

T034 proposes using 'McNemar's Test or Wilcoxon signed-rank'. FR-005 explicitly restricts the statistical test to 'paired t-test or Wilcoxon signed-rank'. Introducing McNemar's Test without a documented justification in the spec constitutes a retroactive narrowing of the allowed methodology, violating the functional requirement.
